# Generation Mechanism

> *This page was translated from [Japanese](../ja/generation.md) by AI.*

This page explains the rules by which mds generates derived code from Markdown.

## Generation Source

The generation sources are code blocks in the `Types`, `Source`, and `Test` sections of implementation Markdown.

mds does not infer implementation from descriptive text. The generated code is determined by code blocks and metadata written in Markdown.

## Generated Files

The generation target is determined based on the target language of the implementation Markdown.

| Input Example | `Source` Example | `Types` Example | `Test` Example |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `src-md/foo/bar.ts.md` | `src/foo/bar.ts` | `src/foo/bar.types.ts` | `tests/foo/bar.test.ts` |
| `src-md/pkg/foo.py.md` | `src/pkg/foo.py` | `src/pkg/foo.pyi` | `tests/pkg/test_foo.py` |
| `src-md/foo/bar.rs.md` | `src/foo/bar.rs` | `src/foo/bar_types.rs` | `tests/foo_bar_test.rs` |

The actual output location is determined by the `roots` configuration in `mds.config.toml` and the language adapter rules.

## Code Block Concatenation

When there are multiple code blocks within `Types`, `Source`, or `Test`, mds concatenates them in order of appearance.

Descriptive text and supplementary headings can be placed between code blocks. Descriptive text is not included in the generated code.

## Dependency Declaration Generation

Dependency declarations such as import, use, and require are generated from the `Uses` table.

Dependency types are handled in the following order:

1. `builtin`
2. `package`
3. `workspace`
4. `internal`

Named dependencies to the same target are merged where possible.

## Generated File Header

Files generated by mds include a management header.

The header contains the source Markdown path and a hash of the generation input.

```text
Generated by mds. Do not edit. Source: <path>. Source-Hash: <sha256>.
```

This header is used to determine whether a generated file is managed by mds.

## Manifest

mds records generation results in `.mds/manifest.toml` within the package.

The manifest contains the generation source, output target, type, hash, and other information.

If the manifest is corrupted, mds will not write generated output for safety.

## Overwrite Rules

mds only overwrites generated files that have a management header.

If an existing file without a management header exists at the generation target, mds raises an error. This prevents accidentally destroying hand-written files.

## Deletion Rules

Even when the source Markdown is removed, mds does not automatically delete old generated files.

Old generated files become targets for warnings and confirmation. Deletion is based on the premise that the user confirms intent before proceeding.

## Diff Confirmation

Before generation, you can confirm differences with `mds build --dry-run`.

```bash
mds build --package path/to/package --dry-run
```

This command does not write files; it displays the planned generation and differences.
